CHOOSE YOUR SERVICE

Experiencing Warning Lights
or Unusual Performance?

Our technicians use advanced diagnostic equipment to identify exactly what's wrong. Pick the service that fits your situation — both are available same day.

SCAN & GO

R99

Fast fault code read while you wait. No appointment needed. Done in 5–10 minutes.

  • OBD-II fault code read
  • Dashboard warning light reset
  • 5–10 minute while-you-wait
  • Verbal result from technician
  • No written report
  • No multi-system deep scan

R99 credited toward Full Diagnostic if you upgrade same day.

MOST POPULAR

FULL DIAGNOSTIC + REPORT

R850

Complete multi-system scan with a full written PDF report. The professional standard.

  • Full ECU computer scan
  • Fault code analysis (all systems)
  • Technician interpretation & assessment
  • Digital or printed diagnostic report
  • Repair recommendations included

Fee applies even if no fault found — you receive a full report regardless.

FAQ

Common Questions

Do I still pay if nothing is found?
Yes. The diagnostic fee applies even if no fault is detected. Technician time and specialised equipment are used regardless of the outcome — just as a doctor charges for a consultation even when tests come back clear.
How long does the diagnostic take?
A standard diagnostic session typically takes 30–60 minutes depending on the number of systems being scanned and the complexity of the fault. We'll give you an estimated time when you book.
What vehicles do you work on?
We work on all makes and models including passenger vehicles, SUVs, bakkies, and trucks. Our diagnostic equipment covers 98% of vehicles manufactured from 1996 onwards.
Can I get the report digitally?
Yes. Your diagnostic report can be sent via WhatsApp or email, or printed on request. The report is useful for getting second quotes, vehicle sales, and insurance purposes.
